description_long: This object is a stovetop espresso maker, commonly known as a moka pot, featuring a faceted, octagonal body made of dark metal. The main body is divided into two chambers, with a visible seam at the midsection, and a spout protrudes from the upper chamber for pouring. A robust, side-mounted handle, likely made of heat-resistant plastic, is attached to the upper section, ergonomically shaped for gripping. The lid is flat with a small, round knob on top, designed for easy lifting, and appears to be removable. The base is flat and wide, providing stability when placed on a surface, and the overall design is utilitarian with no visible decorative patterns. The surface finish is matte, giving the object a subdued, modern appearance.
description: A dark, faceted moka pot with a flat lid, side handle, and spout, featuring a matte finish and utilitarian design. The object is plain, sturdy, and made primarily of metal with a plastic handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout, handle, and knob on the lid clearly, with the faceted body and seam between chambers visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the round knob on the lid and the symmetrical, octagonal shape of the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handle and the plain, faceted surface without the spout visible.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the flat base and the underside of the handle, with the body’s geometric facets apparent.
